task_completion.description: Verify that the agent enables 'Sketch in 3D' mode.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The SolveSpace property browser or active tool state indicates that 'Sketch in 3D' (or 'anywhere in 3D') is selected, rather than a 2D workplane.

task_completion.description: Verify that one vertex of the model is constrained to the global origin.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A vertex of the drawn sketch is locked to the intersection of the X, Y, and Z axes (0,0,0), typically indicated by a coincident constraint at the origin.

task_completion.description: Verify that a wireframe of a tetrahedron is constructed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The canvas shows six line segments connecting four vertices to form a 3D triangular pyramid (tetrahedron).

task_completion.description: Verify that the tetrahedron is regular with an edge length of 50 mm.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A dimension constraint showing '50' (or '50.00') is visible on one of the edges, and equal length constraints are applied to the other edges to ensure it is a regular tetrahedron.

task_completion.description: Verify the model is saved to the specified file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog or the application window title displays the filename 'tetrahedron.slvs' and the directory path '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/'.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the SolveSpace GUI to create the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent interacting with the SolveSpace application window, clicking tools, and drawing in the canvas, rather than using a terminal to generate the file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually constructed the geometry step-by-step.
integrity.visual_evidence: Progressive screenshots show the sequential addition of lines and constraints, proving the model was built from a blank canvas and not just loaded from a pre-existing file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use a text editor to write the .slvs file.
integrity.visual_evidence: No text editors (like nano, vim, or gedit) are visible editing the raw text/XML of a .slvs file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/tetrahedron.slvs'. The expected edge length of the regular tetrahedron is exactly 50.0 mm.
